mnemonics: Universal ∀ is an upside-down A for 'All'. Existential ∃ is a backwards E for 'Exists'.
keyPoints: A universal quantifier (∀) means the statement holds for every element in the domain.
An existential quantifier (∃) means there exists at least one element in the domain for which the statement is true.
The domain of discourse must be defined for the quantifier to have meaning.
commonMistakes: Misplacing the order of quantifiers (e.g., ∀x∃y is not the same as ∃y∀x).
Assuming the negation of a quantified statement simply negates the predicate (e.g., ¬(∀x P(x)) is equivalent to ∃x ¬P(x)).
